2603.20149.title: Enhancing Hyperspace Analogue to Language (HAL) Representations via Attention-Based Pooling for Text Classification
2603.20149.paper_summary: The Hyperspace Analogue to Language (HAL) model relies on global word co-occurrence matrices to construct distributional semantic representations. While these representations capture lexical relationships effectively, aggregating them into sentence-level embeddings via standard mean pooling often results in information loss. Mean pooling assigns equal weight to all tokens, thereby diluting the impact of contextually salient words with uninformative structural tokens. In this paper, we address this limitation by integrating a learnable, temperature-scaled additive attention mechanism into the HAL representation pipeline. To mitigate the sparsity and high dimensionality of the raw co-occurrence matrices, we apply Truncated Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) to project the vectors into a dense latent space prior to the attention layer. We evaluate the proposed architecture on the IMDB sentiment analysis dataset. Empirical results demonstrate that the attention-based pooling approach achieves a test accuracy of 82.38%, yielding an absolute improvement of 6.74 percentage points over the traditional mean pooling baseline (75.64%). Furthermore, qualitative analysis of the attention weights indicates that the mechanism successfully suppresses stop-words and selectively attends to sentiment-bearing tokens, improving both classification performance and model interpretability.
2603.20149.paper_summary_zh: 超空間類似語言（HAL）模型依賴於全球詞彙共現矩陣來構建分佈式語義表示。<lb/>雖然這些表示有效地捕捉了詞彙關係，但通過標準均值池化將它們聚合成句子級嵌入時，往往會導致信息損失。<lb/>均值池化對所有標記賦予相等的權重，從而稀釋了上下文顯著詞的影響，與不具信息性的結構標記混合在一起。<lb/>在本文中，我們通過將可學習的、溫度縮放的加性注意機制整合到HAL表示管道中來解決這一限制。<lb/>為了減輕原始共現矩陣的稀疏性和高維度，我們應用截斷奇異值分解（SVD）將向量投影到注意層之前的密集潛在空間。<lb/>我們在IMDB情感分析數據集上評估所提出的架構。<lb/>實證結果表明，基於注意的池化方法達到了82.38%的測試準確率，相較於傳統均值池化基準（75.64%）提高了6.74個百分點。<lb/>此外，對注意權重的定性分析表明，該機制成功地抑制了停用詞，並選擇性地關注情感承載標記，從而改善了分類性能和模型可解釋性。
2603.20149.author: Ali Sakour et.al.
